description:
Replace `ModuleTypes = Vec<FnTypes>` (positional, one slot per FnIr) with a per-callsite-keyed structure: each `(FnId, input-Descr-tuple)` pair gets its own `FnTypes`. The typer's fixed point runs over (fn, key) pairs.

## Scope

- `ModuleTypes` re-keying.
- `idx_of: HashMap<FnId, usize>` → `HashMap<(FnId, SpecKey), SpecId>` (or staging structure pending .29.2's SpecId introduction).
- Re-shape the typer's fixed point in `type_module` to iterate over (fn, key) pairs. For each Call site, look up callee's FnTypes for the actual arg-Descr tuple; if missing, instantiate it.
- Recursion convergence: seed recursive instances at `any`-return; re-run `type_fn(callee, &input_descrs)`; iterate to fixed point on the (fn, key) graph.

## Out of scope

- Codegen consumption — drivers (`compile_with_backend`) continue to pick the any-key for every FnId in this atom. Cranelift output stays bitwise identical.
- SpecId integer assignment, internal Vec re-keying, multi-Schema emission — all .29.2.

## Acceptance

- Typer fixed point produces `FnTypes` per (FnId, key) pair internally.
- Existing typer unit tests pass unchanged (they index the any-key result).
- `cargo test --release` matrix green.
- CLIF byte-for-byte identical to pre-atom baseline (no codegen change yet — verify by diffing dumped CLIF on the fixture corpus).
title: .29.1: Re-key ModuleTypes to (FnId, input-key)
